subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Decomposing Grass Clippings A(t) — Avg Rate, Derivative Interpretation, Avg Value & Linear Approx
stimulus: Grass clippings are placed in a bin, where they decompose. For $0 \le t \le 30$, the amount of grass clippings remaining in the bin is modeled by $A(t) = 6.687(0.931)^t$, where $A(t)$ is measured in pounds and $t$ is measured in days.

parts.prompt: Find the average rate of change of $A(t)$ over the interval $0 \le t \le 30$. Indicate units of measure.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct answer with units: $\frac{A(30) - A(0)}{30 - 0} \approx -0.197$ (or $-0.196$) pounds per day
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\frac{A(30) - A(0)}{30} \approx -0.197$ lbs/day. Units must be stated.

parts.prompt: Find the value of $A'(15)$. Using correct units, interpret the meaning of the value in the context of the problem.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct value: $A'(15) \approx -0.164$ (or $-0.163$) pounds per day
1 pt: Correct interpretation with units: the amount of grass clippings in the bin is decreasing at a rate of approximately $0.164$ pounds per day at time $t = 15$ days
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $A'(15) \approx -0.164$ lbs/day. Interpretation must reference the rate of change, direction (decreasing), units, and time ($t=15$ days).

parts.prompt: Find the time $t$ for which the amount of grass clippings in the bin is equal to the average amount of grass clippings in the bin over the interval $0 \le t \le 30$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up average value integral: $A(t) = \frac{1}{30}\int_0^{30} A(t)\, dt$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Solves $A(t) = \frac{1}{30}\int_0^{30} A(t)\, dt$ to find $t \approx 12.415$ days.

parts.prompt: For $t > 30$, $L(t)$, the linear approximation to $A$ at $t = 30$, is a better model for the amount of grass clippings remaining in the bin. Use $L(t)$ to predict the time at which there will be 0.5 pound of grass clippings remaining in the bin. Show the work that leads to your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets up linear approximation: $L(t) = A(30) + A'(30)(t - 30)$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $A'(30) \approx -0.056$ and $A(30) \approx 0.783$. $L(t) = 0.5 \Rightarrow t \approx 35.054$ days.
